deployment.title: Dockerfile fails on distroless base due to missing /bin/sh (e.g., when used with Smithery)

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something isn't working

deployment.body:
### Describe the bug

The Dockerfile in this repository uses `gcr.io/distroless/base-debian12` as the base image, which does **not include a shell (`/bin/sh`)**. As a result, any `RUN` step that tries to execute shell commands (e.g., checking for Alpine or installing dependencies) fails. This breaks automated build tools such as [Smithery.ai](https://smithery.ai/) which rely on these steps to work correctly.

### Affected version

Please run ` docker run -i --rm ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server ./github-mcp-server --version` and paste the output below

`Pending output from docker command` *(replace this with actual output)*

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Fork or clone the MCP Server repo.
2. Try to deploy it using [https://smithery.ai](https://smithery.ai)
3. See error due to Dockerfile failure caused by the shell-less base image.

### Expected vs actual behavior

**Expected:** The Dockerfile should work with shell-based commands or use a multi-stage build process that avoids distroless shells during installation steps.

**Actual:** Build fails with an error:
> The Dockerfile in this repository is broken. It attempts to run a shell command (/bin/sh) in the distroless base image...

This makes it incompatible with common CI/CD workflows and server platforms.

### Logs

```
Found Dockerfile in repository.
Could not find smithery.yaml in repository. Generating... (this could take 10 minutes)
Failed to generate missing build config files. Reason:
Error type: build_failure
Reason:
The Dockerfile in this repository is broken. It attempts to run a shell command (/bin/sh) in the distroless base image (gcr.io/distroless/base-debian12), which does not include a shell. This occurs in the RUN step that checks for Alpine Linux and installs additional libraries. Please modify the Dockerfile to either remove or adjust that RUN command (for example, by using a base image that provides a shell or by executing that logic during the build stage instead).
Could not pull or automatically generate required build config files. Please create the build config files manually in your repository and try again.
```

## **User description** Added dockerfile and smithery.yaml to facilitate deployment on smithery ___ ## **CodeAnt-AI Description** - Enhanced summary validation in `manual_summary_shortener.py` to check for unallowed characters and updated directory path handling using `Path`. - Added a `.dockerignore` file to exclude unnecessary directories like `node_modules` and `dist`. - Created a `Dockerfile` to set up a Node.js environment, install dependencies, and expose port 3000 for the application. - Updated the package version in `package.json` from 1.0.3 to 1.0.4. - Added `smithery.yaml` to define deployment configuration, including start command and configuration schema. - Updated summary formats in `modified_magicapi-coder.json` and `modified_pipfeed-parse.json` by removing trailing periods. This PR introduces deployment configurations and enhancements, including Docker support and improved summary validation. These changes facilitate smoother deployment and maintain consistency in API documentation. ___ ## **Changes walkthrough**

deployment.title: Deployment: Dockerfile and Smithery config
deployment.body:
This PR adds files for deploying your MCP server on Smithery. After deployment, users can use your server over WebSockets (hosted on [Smithery](https://smithery.ai)) without needing to install any dependencies.

### Changes
- Added **Dockerfile** to package your server for deployment.
- Added **Smithery Configuration** file to specify how to start your server. See [documentation](https://smithery.ai/docs/deployments).
- Updated **README** to include installation command via Smithery and a popularity badge. _Note that the command only works after the server is deployed on Smithery._

### Server Details
- **Smithery ID**: `@aiyogg/tinypng-mcp-server`
- **Server Homepage**: [https://smithery.ai/server/@aiyogg/tinypng-mcp-server](https://smithery.ai/server/@aiyogg/tinypng-mcp-server?utm_campaign=pr&modal=claim)

### Action Items
- [X] **Build Passing**: We verified that the Docker builds and your server starts up using an automated test.
- [ ] **Code Review**: Please review the changes to ensure the configuration is accurate for your server.
- [ ] **Claim Server**: Head to your [server page](https://smithery.ai/server/@aiyogg/tinypng-mcp-server?utm_campaign=pr&modal=claim) to claim your server. This will let you edit your server listing on Smithery and deploy new versions of your server.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
	- Introduced a container deployment setup that ensures a consistent build and runtime environment.
	- Integrated a command-line configuration for seamless installation that supports required API key settings.
- **Documentation**
	- Enhanced user guides with a new status badge and clear, step-by-step CLI installation instructions.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

ssl_connection.body:
## Overview
**Client:** vscode
**Bug Type:** Server Bug
**Server:** @smithery-ai/slack
**Connection:** Remote
**OS:** Windows
**Source:** Smithery

## Description
Session initialization hang

## Logs
```
(venv_dcnt) D:\workspace\docent\mcp>npx -y @smithery/cli@latest inspect @smithery-ai/slack --verbose
⠋ Resolving @smithery-ai/slack...[verbose] Resolving package @smithery-ai/slack from registry at https://registry.smithery.ai
[verbose] Making GET request to https://registry.smithery.ai/servers/@smithery-ai/slack
⠸ Resolving @smithery-ai/slack...[verbose] Response status: 200
[verbose] Successfully received server data from registry
[verbose] Server @smithery-ai/slack resolved with 2 connection options
[verbose] Resolved server package: @smithery-ai/slack
✔ Successfully resolved @smithery-ai/slack
[verbose] Selected connection type: http
? The Bot User OAuth Token for the Slack app. (required) [REDACTED_SLACK_TOKEN]
? The app token for the Slack app. (optional)
? The signing secret for the Slack app. (optional)
[verbose] Collected Configuration Structure: [
  "token"
]
[verbose] Runtime environment initialized with 11 variables
[verbose] Created transport for server: @smithery-ai/slack
⠏ Connecting to server...2025-05-10T01:46:36.263Z [Runner] Connecting to server: {"id":"@smithery-ai/slack","connectionTypes":["http","ws"]}
2025-05-10T01:46:36.267Z [Runner] Error: API key is required for remote connections
✖ Failed to connect to server: MCP error -32000: Connection closed

(venv_dcnt) D:\workspace\docent\mcp>
```

## Additional Context
import mcp
from mcp.client.websocket import websocket_client
import json
import base64

config = {
    "token": "xoxb-...",
    "appToken": "xapp-1-...",
    "signingSecret": "f51...",
}

# Encode config in base64
config_b64 = base64.b64encode(json.dumps(config).encode())
smithery_api_key = "..."

# Create server URL
url = f"wss://server.smithery.ai/@smithery-ai/slack/ws?config={config_b64}&api_key={smithery_api_key}"


async def main():
    # Connect to the server using websocket client
    async with websocket_client(url) as streams:
        async with mcp.ClientSession(*streams) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize() # It hangs at this point!
            # List available tools
            tools_result = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {', '.join([t.name for t in tools_result.tools])}")

            # Example of calling a tool:
            # result = await session.call_tool("tool-name", arguments={"arg1": "value"})


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio

    asyncio.run(main())


## Screenshots
*Screenshots can be added by dragging and dropping them here.*

ssl_connection.title: Subject: HTTP 500 Proxy Errors - Smithery Toolbox MCP Server Connection Failures
ssl_connection.body:
## Overview
**Client:** cursor
**Bug Type:** Smithery Bug
**Server:** @smithery/toolbox
**Connection:** Remote
**OS:** Windows

## Description
Subject: HTTP 500 Proxy Errors - Smithery Toolbox MCP Server Connection Failures
Hi Smithery Support,
I'm experiencing connection issues with the Smithery toolbox MCP server. My application is unable to connect and receiving HTTP 500 errors when trying to proxy requests.
Error Details:

Server instance: smithery-4cb6a5a2-dfc3-4b84-b8ac-e81fa301f9aa.fly.dev
Error: "Error occurred while trying to proxy" (HTTP 500)
Timestamp: June 9, 2025, starting around 06:58 UTC
Both initial connection attempts and heartbeat pings are failing

Impact:

MCP requests timing out after ~1 minute
Unable to use toolbox functionality
Clean disconnections occurring due to proxy failures

Could you please check if there are any issues with this specific server instance or the proxy infrastructure? The error suggests the problem is on the server side rather than my client configuration.
API Key reference (last 4 chars): 03b7
Thanks for your help!

## Screenshots
*Screenshots can be added by dragging and dropping them here.*

ssl_connection.title: [Smithery Bug] @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server
ssl_connection.body:
## Overview
**Client:** claude
**Bug Type:** Smithery Bug
**Server:** @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server
**Connection:** Remote
**OS:** Windows

## Description
Claude thinks it creates the docx file, but nothing appears. To fix you to manually specify a directory claude has access to in claude_desktop_config.json. 

## Steps to Reproduce
install via standard smithery copy paste command line install
ask claude to create docx document
document is never created locally

## Logs
```
 npx -y @smithery/cli@latest inspect @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server --verbose
⠋ Resolving @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server...[verbose] Loading settings from C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\smithery
[verbose] Reading settings file: C:\Users\user\AppData\Roaming\smithery\settings.json
[verbose] Resolving package @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server using Smithery SDK at <default>
[verbose] Settings file content loaded
[verbose] Settings parsed: {
  "userId": "54431b83-14a8-46f4-8386-70cc6152674e",
  "analyticsConsent": false,
  "askedConsent": true,
  "cache": {
    "servers": {}
  }
}
⠼ Resolving @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server...[verbose] Successfully received server data from Smithery SDK
[verbose] Resolved server package: @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server
✔ Successfully resolved @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server
[verbose] Selected connection type: http
[verbose] Collected Configuration Structure: []
[verbose] Runtime environment initialized with 11 variables
[verbose] Created transport for server: @GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server
⠸ Connecting to server...2025-05-26T01:38:13.456Z [Runner] Connecting to server: {"id":"@GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server","connectionTypes":["http"]}
2025-05-26T01:38:13.457Z [Runner] Error: API key is required for remote connections
✖ Failed to connect to server: MCP error -32000: Connection closed
```

## Additional Context
SOLUTION: Modify claude_desktop_config.json as per bellow. However it would seem this removes the API key, but it still works, so i guess its functioning locally?

```
"Office-Word-MCP-Server": {
	  "command": "uvx",
	  "args": [
		"--directory",
		"C:\\path\\to\\claude\\documents",
		"--from",
		"office-word-mcp-server",
		"word_mcp_server.exe"
	  ]
}
```

## Screenshots
*Screenshots can be added by dragging and dropping them here.*

ssl_connection.title: [Windows] MCP server terminated immediately with "Client closed" when using recommended cmd /c npx config
ssl_connection.body:
# [Windows] MCP server terminated immediately with "Client closed" when using official cmd /c npx config

## Describe the bug

When launching a [MCP-compatible server](https://github.com/drumnation/unsplash-smart-mcp-server) via `mcp.json` in **Cursor on Windows**, the server exits immediately with a `"Client closed"` error, despite the command being configured per official recommendations:

```json
"command": "cmd",
"args": ["/c", "npx", "tsx", "src/server.ts"]
```

This issue prevents the MCP server from being recognized and used in Cursor.

---

## Expected behavior

The server should:
- Remain running as a long-lived, stdio-based MCP process;
- Appear with tools listed in Cursor’s UI;
- Be accessible as a backend toolchain.

---

## Actual behavior

Upon clicking the **refresh icon** in the Cursor MCP panel:
- Cursor tries to run: `cmd /c npx tsx src/server.ts`

- Output:
  ```
  Starting new stdio process with command: cmd /c npx tsx src/server.ts
  Client closed for command
  Error in MCP: Client closed
  Failed to reload client: MCP error -32000: Connection closed
  ```

- In the Cursor panel:
  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/674e1fb5-59da-4ac5-8e94-7dc1c19cb9e5)
  - 🔴 Red status indicator
  - ❌ Message: `Client closed`
  - ⛔ No tools are listed

---

## Environment

| Component       | Version         |
|----------------|-----------------|
| OS             | Windows 11 Pro x64 |
| Node.js        | v20.x |
| npm            | v10.9.0 |
| tsx            | v4.19.3 |
| Cursor         | v0.48.7 |
| MCP Server     | https://github.com/drumnation/unsplash-smart-mcp-server |

---

## What works fine

✅ Running the server manually from CMD:

```bash
npm run dev
```

Output:
```
√ Connected, server capabilities: logging, tools
√ Pick a primitive » tool(stock_photo)
...
[server log]: {
  message: 'Searching Unsplash for "professional background" (1 images)'
}
```

✅ Running integration tests via `npm test`:

```bash
> tsx --test src/__tests__/**/*.test.ts
✔ All tests passed (6/6)
```

✅ Server remains alive when launched manually:
- MCP handshake completes.
- Tools function as expected.
- STDIO communication behaves correctly.

---

## Attempts to resolve

- ❌ Switching `command` from `cmd` to `npx` directly (`"command": "npx"`) results in the **same issue**: process is marked as closed.
- ❌ Removing `cmd` entirely causes the command not to work on Windows due to terminal behavior.
- ✅ Confirmed `createServer()` is properly used from `@mcp/pkg`, and STDIO remains open when outside Cursor.

---

## Suspected Cause

MCP process management in Cursor may be misinterpreting the lifecycle of Windows subprocesses launched via `cmd /c`, even though the child process (`npx tsx`) is still running and interactive via STDIO.

This could stem from:
- `cmd` wrapper exiting and closing parent handles;
- STDIO buffering/stream behavior in Node subprocess on Windows;
- Premature determination that the process exited, even though server is alive.

---

## Suggested Fix / Request

- Investigate how MCP determines whether a Windows MCP server is "closed";
- Consider more robust handling of `cmd /c` behavior under Windows;
- Provide verbose/debug logs for MCP startup sequence;
- Optionally allow `"shell": true` or `detached` options in config.

---

## Repro Repo

Use this project:  
🔗 https://github.com/drumnation/unsplash-smart-mcp-server  
Configured with:
```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

MCP entry point:
```ts
// src/server.ts
import { createServer } from "@mcp/pkg";
import { tools } from "./tools";

createServer({ tools });
```

> This sets up a compliant stdio-based MCP server using `@mcp/pkg`, which works properly when run directly, but fails when launched via MCP config inside Cursor on Windows.

---

Thanks again for all your work on Cursor and MCP! This toolchain is amazing — looking forward to seeing MCP stabilized for Windows environments 🙏

ssl_connection.title: Add Return Track and Mixing Controls to Ableton MCP
ssl_connection.body:
### **User description** ## Overview This PR adds essential mixing capabilities to the Ableton MCP system, focusing on return tracks and volume controls. These features enable more comprehensive mixing workflows directly through the MCP API. ## Features Added ### 1. Return Track Creation - Implemented `_create_return_track` function in the AbletonMCP_Remote_Script - Exposed the functionality through the MCP Server API as `create_return_track` - Enhanced the `_get_track_by_index` function to handle both regular tracks and return tracks - Created a test script (`return_track_test.py`) to verify the functionality ### 2. Send Controls - Implemented `_set_send_level` function to control send levels from tracks to return tracks - Exposed the functionality through the MCP Server API as `set_send_level` - Created a test script (`send_control_test.py`) to verify the functionality - This completes the return track workflow by allowing tracks to send audio to return tracks ### 3. Volume Controls - Implemented `_set_track_volume` function to control track volume levels - Added `_linear_to_db` helper function to convert linear volume values (0.0-1.0) to dB - Exposed the functionality through the MCP Server API as `set_track_volume` - Created a test script ([volume_control_test.py](cci:7://file:///Users/youssefhemimy/Documents/ableton-mcp/volume_control_test.py:0:0-0:0)) to verify the functionality - Provides intelligent volume control with proper dB conversion: - 0.0 → -∞ dB (muted) - 0.85 → 0 dB (unity gain) - 1.0 → +6 dB (maximum) ## Testing All features have been thoroughly tested with dedicated test scripts that demonstrate the functionality and verify correct behavior. You can find tests folder with all the tests. ___ ### **PR Type** Enhancement, Tests, Documentation ___ ### **Description** - Added comprehensive support for return tracks, send level, and track volume controls in Ableton MCP, including dB conversion and improved device/EQ parameter handling. - Exposed new mixing and device control features through the MCP Server API, with enhanced command routing and error handling. - Introduced detailed test scripts for return track creation/manipulation, send level control, volume control, precise EQ Eight parameter control, audio effects device parameter control, and browser/device management. - Added extensive documentation covering device parameter control, existing system capabilities, and an implementation/testing summary. - Improved error handling, logging, and documentation throughout the codebase. ___ ### **Changes walkthrough** 📝

- **New Features**
  - Introduced a comprehensive set of tools for advanced GitHub Projects V2 management, including creating, updating, archiving projects, managing items, fields, views, and task workflows.
  - Added tools for bulk-adding issues, converting drafts to issues, grouping tasks, and managing task status with optional comments.
  - Implemented a test connection tool to verify GitHub API access and token validity.

- **Documentation**
  - Expanded and restructured documentation, including detailed README, usage guide, and a comprehensive markdown overview of the GitHub Projects V2 server.
  - Added environment variable example files for GitHub Projects and GitHub integrations.

- **Chores**
  - Updated package metadata for rebranding and versioning.
  - Revised TypeScript configuration and streamlined .gitignore.
  - Added and updated scripts for build and permission management.

- **Refactor**
  - Replaced and reorganized internal modules for improved error handling, logging, token validation, and utility functions.
  - Migrated to new client and tool implementations for GitHub API interactions.

- **Revert**
  - Removed legacy code, configuration files, and outdated utility modules related to previous implementations.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Release notes

v9.28.0

Features

  • b0674be feat: Customization of serialization for languageOptions (#19760) (Nicholas C. Zakas)

Bumps [eslint](https://github.com/eslint/eslint) and [@types/eslint](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/tree/HEAD/types/eslint). These dependencies needed to be updated together. Updates `eslint` from 9.7.0 to 9.28.0
Release notes

Sourced from eslint's releases.

v9.28.0

Features

  • b0674be feat: Customization of serialization for languageOptions (#19760) (Nicholas C. Zakas)
  • a95721f feat: Add --pass-on-unpruned-suppressions CLI option (#19773) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • bfd0e7a feat: support TypeScript syntax in no-use-before-define (#19566) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • 68c61c0 feat: support TS syntax in no-shadow (#19565) (Nitin Kumar)
  • 0f773ef feat: support TS syntax in no-magic-numbers (#19561) (Nitin Kumar)
  • c4a6b60 feat: add allowTypeAnnotation to func-style (#19754) (sethamus)
  • b03ad17 feat: add TypeScript support to prefer-arrow-callback (#19678) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • bc3c331 feat: ignore overloaded function declarations in func-style rule (#19755) (sethamus)

Bug Fixes

  • eea3e7e fix: Remove configured global variables from GlobalScope#implicit (#19779) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • a467de3 fix: update context.report types (#19751) (Nitin Kumar)
  • fd467bb fix: remove interopDefault to use jiti's default (#19697) (sethamus)
  • 72d16e3 fix: avoid false positive in no-unassigned-vars for declare module (#19746) (Azat S.)
  • 81c3c93 fix: curly types (#19750) (Eli)

Documentation

  • 3ec2082 docs: Nested arrays in files config entry (#19799) (Nicholas C. Zakas)

  • 52f5b7a docs: fix minor typos and add links (#19743) (루밀LuMir)
  • 00716a3 docs: upfront recommend against using the no-return-await rule (#19727) (Mike DiDomizio)

Chores

  • 175b7b8 chore: upgrade to @eslint/js@9.28.0 (#19802) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 844f5a6 chore: package.json update for @​eslint/js release (Jenkins)

  • 0f49329 refactor: use a service to emit warnings (#19725) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 20a9e59 chore: update dependency shelljs to ^0.10.0 (#19740) (renovate[bot])

v9.27.0

Features

  • d71e37f feat: Allow flags to be set in ESLINT_FLAGS env variable (#19717) (Nicholas C. Zakas)
  • ba456e0 feat: Externalize MCP server (#19699) (Nicholas C. Zakas)
  • 07c1a7e feat: add allowRegexCharacters to no-useless-escape (#19705) (sethamus)
  • 7bc6c71 feat: add no-unassigned-vars rule (#19618) (Jacob Bandes-Storch)
  • ee40364 feat: convert no-array-constructor suggestions to autofixes (#19621) (sethamus)
  • 32957cd feat: support TS syntax in max-params (#19557) (Nitin Kumar)

Bug Fixes

  • 5687ce7 fix: correct mismatched removed rules (#19734) (루밀LuMir)

... (truncated)

container_runtime.title: NexusMind_Neo4j

labels.name: enhancement
labels.description: New feature or request

container_runtime.body:
feat: Complete Neo4j-native architecture refactor and repository cleanup

This commit consolidates the full transition of the NexusMind MCP server to a Neo4j-native architecture, along with comprehensive repository cleanup and documentation updates.

**Phase 1 & 2: Neo4j Integration & Refactoring**
- Replaced the in-memory ASRGoTGraph with direct Neo4j backend integration.
- All 8 processing stages (Initialization to Reflection) have been refactored to perform CRUD and query operations directly against Neo4j using a new `neo4j_utils` service.
- GoTProcessor has been updated to orchestrate Neo4j-native stages, eliminating in-memory graph management and persistence logic.
- The `ASRGoTGraph` class and `graph_state.py` file have been removed.
- A Neo4j service has been added to `docker-compose.yml` for development and testing.
- Initial data seeding from Neo4j has been implemented in the InitializationStage.

**Phase 3: Repository Review, Pruning, and Documentation**
- Numerous outdated and unnecessary files and scripts have been pruned from `c__/`, `scripts/`, `simple_tests/`, `test/`, and the root directory.
- A new `graph_analysis_neo4j.py` has been drafted with placeholders for GDS utilities.
- The `README.md` has been updated with new sections: Architecture Overview, Setup/Installation (including Neo4j & WSL2), Running the Application, Data Flow, Testing, and Dependencies.
- Created content for `TESTING.MD` (from a previous draft) outlining unit, integration (with Testcontainers), and E2E testing strategies.
- Drafted a comprehensive entry for `CHANGELOG.md` detailing these major changes.
- Reviewed other .md files for relevance and necessary updates.

This architectural overhaul enhances persistence, scalability, and enables powerful graph-based operations directly within Neo4j, establishing it as the single source of truth.

container_runtime.title: Add comprehensive smithery.yaml configuration file
container_runtime.body:
This PR adds a comprehensive `smithery.yaml` configuration file for the MCP Python template project.

## Changes Made

- Created a detailed `smithery.yaml` configuration file that includes:
  - **MCP Server Configuration**: Defines the server name, transport method, and available commands
  - **Command Documentation**: Detailed specifications for the `hello` and `add` commands with parameters
  - **Runtime Configuration**: Python version requirements and entry point
  - **Dependencies**: Lists required packages from pyproject.toml
  - **Build Configuration**: References the existing Dockerfile
  - **Development Settings**: Hot reload and debug configurations
  - **Metadata**: Project information, keywords, and repository details
  - **Health Check**: Basic health monitoring configuration

## Features Configured

### MCP Commands:
1. **hello** - Greeting command that takes a name parameter
2. **add** - Mathematical operation that adds two integers

### Technical Specifications:
- Python >=3.12 runtime
- FastMCP server implementation
- stdio transport protocol
- Docker containerization support

This configuration makes the project fully compatible with Smithery tooling and provides clear documentation for all available MCP server capabilities.

## Testing
The configuration follows the existing project structure and maintains compatibility with the current codebase.

authentication.title: bug: split function not found when subtask variable is empty

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Something isn't working

authentication.body:
### Description


### Steps to Reproduce

When running this command in CLI:
`npx task-master add-task --prompt="Add task to..." --dependencies=18`

...I receive this output:

```bash
  Dependency chain visualization:
  └── Task 18: Implement Secure Certificate Validati...
      ├── Task 4: Create Configuration Management System
      │   └── Task 3: Implement Protocol-Safe Logging System
      └── Task 6: Setup Authentication and Secret Manag...


╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ AI Task Generation                                        │
│                                                           │
│ Analyzing context and generating task details using AI... │
│ Context size: 6,680 characters                            │
│ Dependency detection: Explicit dependencies               │
│ Detailed tasks: 6                                         │
│                                                           │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

- Generating new task with Main AI...
/ Generating new task with Main AI...
√ Task generated successfully
[INFO] Generating task files...
[INFO] Preparing to regenerate 29 task files
[INFO] Validating and fixing dependencies
[INFO] Checking for orphaned task files to clean up...
[INFO] No orphaned task files found
[INFO] Generating individual task files...
[ERROR] Error generating task files: (subtask.details || "").split is not a function
Error generating task files: (subtask.details || "").split is not a function
```

### Expected Behavior

This is a project that was started fresh with the new 0.16.2 code, and the error is in the same version. I've been working with this project for about 12 hours at this point with no issues. This first appeared when attempting to add the task through the MCP server, but the steps to reproduce are clearer with the CLI approach.

### Actual Behavior

Some value is being returned by subtask.details that's truthy enough to not fall into the empty string case ahead of .split()

### Screenshots or Logs

Error from MCP tool call in Cursor: 
Error: (subtask.details || "").split is not a function
Version: 0.16.2
Name: task-master-ai

### Environment

- Task Master version: 0.16.2
- Node.js version:  24.1.0
- Operating system: Windows
- IDE (if applicable): Cursor 1.0.1

### Additional Context

Just started, so may be transient. I've tried twice about a half-hour apart, and have gotten the failure about 10 times between the CLI and IDE. 

If it auto-resolves, it might suggest that there's some defensive coding needed in this area.

other.title: WSL2 + Cursor MCP fails to create client: Failed to create client error
other.body:
Hello,

I'm encountering an issue when running MCP (`@waldzellai/clear-thought`) on WSL with Cursor IDE. The MCP fails with the error message:

```
❌ Failed to create client
```

## Environment
- **OS:** Windows 11 with WSL2 (Ubuntu 22.04)
- **Smithery CLI version:** `@smithery/cli@latest` via `npx`
- **MCP:** `@waldzellai/clear-thought`
- **Command:**
  ```sh
  npx -y @smithery/cli@latest run @waldzellai/clear-thought --key=[REDACTED]
  ```

## Screenshot
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/832f64ca-d735-4989-a2a6-ddf0ed84e433)

## Notes
- Other MCPs might work fine; this error appears specific to `clear-thought`.
- The key and command seem valid.
- WSL networking and permissions are functioning for other Node apps.

Let me know if there’s any additional debugging I can do on my end.

Thanks!

other.title: Fix ModuleNotFoundError and update mypy.ini

labels.name: enhancement
labels.description: New feature or request

other.body:
This commit addresses issues identified after the initial refactor:

1.  **Fix ModuleNotFoundError:**
    *   Removed an obsolete import of `ASRGoTGraph` from `src/asr_got_reimagined/api/routes/mcp.py`.
    *   Removed the associated dead code block in `handle_asr_got_query` that referenced `ASRGoTGraph`.
    This resolves a server startup error.

2.  **Update mypy.ini:**
    *   Changed `python_version` from `3.13` to `3.11` to align with the project's specified Python compatibility range.
    *   Ensured `files = src/` is active for explicit type checking of the source directory.

These changes are appended to the work done in the initial refactor and cleanup commit.

other.title: [docs]: refactor TableOfContents component locations and imports
other.body:
## Summary

Refactored the documentation TableOfContents components to follow a consistent location structure. All TableOfContents components are now located at `packages/docs/docs/[package-name]/table-of-contents.tsx` instead of `packages/docs/components/TableOfContents/[package-name].tsx`.

## Changes Made

### File Movements
- **Moved 17 TableOfContents components** from `packages/docs/components/TableOfContents/[package-name].tsx` to `packages/docs/docs/[package-name]/table-of-contents.tsx`
- **Created new directories** for packages that didn't have docs folders (bundler, cloudrun, gif, lambda, etc.)

### Updated Components
- `animation-utils.tsx` → `animation-utils/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `bundler.tsx` → `bundler/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `cli.tsx` → `cli/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `cli/browser.tsx` → `cli/browser/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `cloudrun.tsx` → `cloudrun/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `gif.tsx` → `gif/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `lambda.tsx` → `lambda/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `LayoutUtils.tsx` → `layout-utils/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `lottie.tsx` → `lottie/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `media-utils.tsx` → `media-utils/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `motion-blur.tsx` → `motion-blur/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `noise.tsx` → `noise/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `paths.tsx` → `paths/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `preload.tsx` → `preload/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `remotion.tsx` → `remotion/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `rive.tsx` → `rive/table-of-contents.tsx`
- `shapes.tsx` → `shapes/table-of-contents.tsx`

### Import Updates
- **Updated `api.tsx`** with new import paths for all moved components
- **Updated 22 .mdx files** throughout the documentation with corrected import paths:
  - Changed `'../../components/TableOfContents/[package-name]'` to `'../../[package-name]/table-of-contents'`
  - Changed `'../components/TableOfContents/[package-name]'` to `'../[package-name]/table-of-contents'`
- **Fixed all TypeScript imports** in moved components to reference Grid and TOCItem from correct relative paths

### What Remained Unchanged
- **Core components** (Grid.tsx, TOCItem.tsx, api.tsx, pro-label.tsx) remain in `components/TableOfContents/`
- **Transitions sub-components** (presentations.tsx, timings.tsx) remain in their original location as they are shared utilities, not main TableOfContents components

## Benefits

1. **Consistent Structure**: All TableOfContents components now follow the pattern `docs/[package-name]/table-of-contents.tsx`
2. **Better Organization**: Components are co-located with their respective package documentation
3. **Cleaner Imports**: Documentation files can now import TableOfContents with shorter, more intuitive paths
4. **Maintainability**: Easier to find and maintain TableOfContents components alongside their related documentation

## Verification

- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes
- ✅ ESLint passes
- ✅ All imports resolved correctly
- ✅ No breaking changes to functionality

other.title: Meta: Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

labels.name: request-failed
labels.description: Meta issue to collect failed request scenarios

other.body:
This meta issue tracks scenarios where chat requests fail, with a variety of potential causes from the user's machine to a service disruption (especially for _preview_ models).

Error message:
> Sorry, your request failed. Please try again. …

Here are some potential workarounds you can try:
- Retry the request (🔄).
- Retry the request with a different model.
- Start a new chat (➕).
- Check your Copilot Chat output logs for details (`CTRL + SHIFT + U` or Command `Output: Show Output Channels…`, and then select `GitHub Copilot Chat`).

If the issue persists, please comment and attach the logs. We are continuously monitoring and improving service reliability, and any reports with logs will help us better understand and resolve potential causes.

other.title: Fix server cold start issue by implementing automatic retry logic
other.body:
## Problem

Users had to press the analyze button twice due to server cold starts on Render. The backend server spins down during periods of inactivity, causing the first request to timeout or fail while the server boots up, requiring a manual second attempt.

## Solution

Implemented automatic retry logic that detects server cold start scenarios and retries the request transparently:

- **Automatic Detection**: Identifies timeout errors (`AbortError`) and 500 server errors that typically indicate cold starts
- **Single Retry**: Automatically retries once after 3 seconds to avoid infinite loops
- **User Feedback**: Shows "Server is starting up, retrying automatically..." message and "Retrying..." button state
- **Smart Error Handling**: Only retries on server-related errors, preserves original error handling for genuine failures
- **Dual Mode Support**: Works for both single subreddit and bulk analysis modes

## Changes Made

- Added `isRetrying` state to track retry status
- Modified `handleSingleSubmit` and `handleBulkSubmit` to accept `isRetry` parameter
- Implemented server error detection logic that checks for `AbortError` and "Error 500" messages
- Enhanced loading states to show retry progress
- Updated button text to display "Retrying..." during retry attempts

## Testing

- Validated retry condition logic works correctly for various error types
- Confirmed retry only triggers once per request
- Verified build passes successfully
- Tested that genuine errors (404, network issues) don't trigger unnecessary retries

This eliminates the frustrating "double-click" experience while maintaining robust error handling for actual failures.

Fixes #7.

---

other.title: feat: improve search
other.body:
BREAKING: None - all existing functionality preserved with backward compatibility

Summary:
- Add intelligent content type and schema caching to eliminate repeated API calls
- Implement smart search across multiple content types with single tool call
- Add query builder for automatic GraphQL query generation
- Reduce search workflow from 3+ manual tool calls to 1 automated call
- Improve search latency from 3-5s to ~500ms after cache load

Features:
* Content type and schema caching with 5-minute refresh cycle
* Smart search tool that automatically searches across all text fields
* Query builder tool for learning and debugging GraphQL queries
* Enhanced existing handlers to use cache-first with API fallback
* Comprehensive error handling and graceful degradation

Technical Details:
* Cache stored in memory with configurable refresh intervals
* Parallel loading of content type schemas during startup
* Automatic detection of searchable text fields (String without !)
* Support for content type filtering and result limiting
* Zero additional environment variables required

Performance Impact:
* Zero-latency metadata access after initial cache load
* Reduced API calls to Contentful backend by ~80% for common operations
* Memory usage: <1MB for typical Contentful spaces
* Startup time: +2-5s for initial cache load

Files Modified:
* src/handlers/graphql-handlers.ts - Core caching and smart search logic
* src/index.ts - Startup integration and tool registration
* src/types/tools.ts - New tool definitions
* test/ - Comprehensive test suite with >95% coverage
* SMART_QUERY.md - Implementation documentation

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>lint-staged/lint-staged (lint-staged)</summary>

### [`v16.1.1`](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1611)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/compare/v16.1.0...v16.1.1)

##### Patch Changes

-   [#​1565](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/pull/1565) [`3686977`](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/commit/3686977ccdadf70b709c16c0346ef6c2b18e2376) Thanks [@​iiroj](https://redirect.github.com/iiroj)! - *Lint-staged* now explicitly warns about potential data loss when using `--no-stash`.

-   [#​1571](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/pull/1571) [`02299a9`](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/commit/02299a9e4f98a05c13beb27f3596af73aaa8c150) Thanks [@​iiroj](https://redirect.github.com/iiroj)! - Function tasks (introduced in v16.0.0) only received the staged files matching the conpmnfigured glob, instead of all staged files.

-   [#​1563](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/pull/1563) [`bc61c74`](https://redirect.github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged/commit/bc61c74383b6d100c55b8d275b979d583ffbe5a1) Thanks [@​iiroj](https://redirect.github.com/iiroj)! - This version fixes incorrect behavior where unstaged changes were committed when using the `--no-stash` option. This happened because `--no-stash` implied `--no-hide-partially-staged`, meaning unstaged changes to files which also had other staged changes were added to the commit by *lint-staged*; this is no longer the case.

    The previous (incorrect) behavior can still be achieved by using both options `--no-stash --no-hide-partially-staged` at the same time.

</details>

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Change | Age | Adoption | Passing | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [eslint-config-prettier](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier) | [`^9.1.0` -> `^10.1.5`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/eslint-config-prettier/9.1.0/10.1.5) | [![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/eslint-config-prettier/10.1.5?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![adoption](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/adoption/npm/eslint-config-prettier/10.1.5?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![passing](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/compatibility/npm/eslint-config-prettier/9.1.0/10.1.5?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/eslint-config-prettier/9.1.0/10.1.5?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>prettier/eslint-config-prettier (eslint-config-prettier)</summary>

### [`v10.1.5`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1015)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.1.4...v10.1.5)

##### Patch Changes

-   [#​332](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/332) [`60fef02`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/60fef02574467d31d10ff47ecb567d378483c9d4) Thanks [@​JounQin](https://redirect.github.com/JounQin)! - chore: add `funding` field into `package.json`

### [`v10.1.4`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1014)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.1.3...v10.1.4)

##### Patch Changes

-   [#​328](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/328) [`94b4799`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/94b47999e7eb13b703835729331376cef598b850) Thanks [@​silvenon](https://redirect.github.com/silvenon)! - fix(cli): do not crash on no rules configured

### [`v10.1.3`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1013)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.1.2...v10.1.3)

##### Patch Changes

-   [#​325](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/325) [`4e95a1d`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/4e95a1d50073f1a24f004239ad6e1a4ffa8476df) Thanks [@​pilikan](https://redirect.github.com/pilikan)! - fix: this package is `commonjs`, align its types correctly

### [`v10.1.2`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1012)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.1.1...v10.1.2)

##### Patch Changes

-   [#​321](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/321) [`a8768bf`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/a8768bfe54a91d08f0cef8705f91de2883436bb0) Thanks [@​Fdawgs](https://redirect.github.com/Fdawgs)! - chore(package): add homepage for some 3rd-party registry - see [#​321](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/321) for more details

### [`v10.1.1`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1011)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.1.0...v10.1.1)

##### Patch Changes

-   [#​309](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/309) [`eb56a5e`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/eb56a5e09964e49045bccde3c616275eb0a0902d) Thanks [@​JounQin](https://redirect.github.com/JounQin)! - fix: separate the `/flat` entry for compatibility

    For flat config users, the previous `"eslint-config-prettier"` entry still works, but `"eslint-config-prettier/flat"` adds a new `name` property for [config-inspector](https://eslint.org/blog/2024/04/eslint-config-inspector/), we just can't add it for the default entry for compatibility.

    See also [#​308](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/issues/308)

    ```ts
    // before
    import eslintConfigPrettier from "eslint-config-prettier";

    // after
    import eslintConfigPrettier from "eslint-config-prettier/flat";
    ```

### [`v10.1.0`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1010)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.0.3...v10.1.0)

-   [#​280](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/280) [`cba5737`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/cba57377e4c86d20d17042d6999eabc754fddc03) Thanks [@​zanminkian](https://redirect.github.com/zanminkian)! - feat: add declaration file

### [`v10.0.1`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/releases/tag/v10.0.1)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v10.0.0...v10.0.1)

### eslint-config-prettier

#### 10.0.1

#### What's Changed

-   chore: migrate to changeset for automatically releasing by [@​JounQin](https://redirect.github.com/JounQin) in [https://github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/278](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/278)
-   add support for `@stylistic/eslint-plugin` by [@​abrahamguo](https://redirect.github.com/abrahamguo) in [https://github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/272](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/272)

#### New Contributors

-   [@​JounQin](https://redirect.github.com/JounQin) made their first contribution in [https://github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/278](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/278)
-   [@​abrahamguo](https://redirect.github.com/abrahamguo) made their first contribution in [https://github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/272](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/272)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v9.1.0...v10.0.1

### [`v10.0.0`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#1000)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/compare/v9.1.0...v10.0.0)

##### Major Changes

-   [#​272](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/pull/272) [`5be64be`](https://redirect.github.com/prettier/eslint-config-prettier/commit/5be64bef68c3a9bf7202f591f54ffec02572e46b) Thanks [@​abrahamguo](https://redirect.github.com/abrahamguo)! - add support for [@​stylistic](https://redirect.github.com/stylistic) formatting rules

</details>

other.body:
Bumps [github.com/pulumi/pulumi/sdk/v3](https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi) from 3.153.1 to 3.177.0.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/pulumi/pulumi/sdk/v3's releases.

v3.177.0

3.177.0 (2025-06-13)

Features

  • [sdk/yaml] Enable support for views by default #19847

Bug Fixes

  • [auto/nodejs] Fix stack name in object after rename operations #19836

  • [cli] Respect --generate-only when converting local SDKs

v3.176.0

3.176.0 (2025-06-12)

Features

  • [engine] Enable support for views by default #19813

  • [cli/install] Resolve plugin download URLs by referencing the Pulumi Registry #19762

  • [cli/plugin] Improve the error message when installing unknown resource plugins #19773

  • [sdk/go] Add experimental policy as code SDK #19670

Bug Fixes

  • [cli] Fix package add always reporting providers as crashing #19769

  • [programgen] Fix panic in programgen

  • [cli/display] Send output about installing policy packs to stderr instead of stdout #19816

  • [sdk/{go,nodejs,python}] Fix use of parameterized packages in mocks #19722

... (truncated)

other.title: composer (master)(deps-dev): update rector/rector requirement from ^2.0.17 to ^2.0.18

labels.name: dependencies
labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file

other.body:
Updates the requirements on [rector/rector](https://github.com/rectorphp/rector) to permit the latest version.
Release notes

Sourced from rector/rector's releases.

Released: Rector 2.0.18

New Features :partying_face:

  • Bump to PHP-Parser ^5.5.0 (#6946)
  • [DeadCode] Add RemoveArgumentFromDefaultParentCallRector (#6949)
  • [DeadCode] Add RemoveDeadCatchRector rule (#6939), Thanks @​P3gucka!

Bugfixes :bug:

  • [Php80] Add more exact Expr node that always bool for switch(true) usage on ChangeSwitchToMatchRector (#6965)
  • [Php80] Prevent adding empty return when stringable always throws (#6968), Thanks @​Mika-!
  • [php 8.0] Check if class is an attribute in AnnotationToAttributeRector, to avoid non-attribute bug (#6954)
  • [Transform] Fix FQCN usage on new name on MergeInterfacesRector (#6956)
  • Rectify (#6957)
  • [Printer] Skip change new line as space on anonymous class on print (#6958)
  • [Printer] Use regex string new space detection for anonymous class (#6959)
  • [Php80] Handle dynamic bool expr on ChangeSwitchToMatchRector (#6961)
  • [Php80] Do not change to match(true) on defined case cond on ChangeSwitchToMatchRector (#6962)
  • [Php80] Do not change to match(true) on variable from param on case cond on ChangeSwitchToMatchRector (#6963)
  • Fix fixture file name (#6964)
  • [Renaming] [Symfony 7.0] Skip ClassConstFetch that rename its ->class to Interface that constant not exists in new interface (#6966)
  • Fix stub (#6967)
  • [DeadCode] Clean up useless union StmtsAwareInterface|ClassMethod on TerminatedNodeAnalyzer (#6969)
  • [Php80] Handle has silent void on StringableForToStringRector (#6970)
  • [PostRector] Clean up rename process on ClassRenamingPostRector (#6971)
  • [DeadCode] Early check skip no extends on RemoveDefaultArgumentFromParentCallRector (#6950)
  • [DeadCode] Allow remove createMock method call on RemoveUnusedVariableAssignRector (#6951)
  • [DeadCode] Skip when next catch is parent class exception with special treatment over just throw on RemoveDeadCatchRector (#6952)

rectorphp/rector-symfony :musical_note:

  • [Symfony 7.3] Handle commands without configure method in InvokableCommandInputAttributeRector (#797), Thanks @​gharlan
  • [Symfony 7.3] Handle fluent call addArgument and addOption on InvokableCommandInputAttributeRector (#795)
  • Twig attributes: add required first param (#793), Thanks @​gharlan
  • Fix namespace of console Option attribute (#792), Thanks @​gharlan
  • fix: Normalize variable names by replacing hyphens with underscores in Symfony73 command argument and option processing (#790), Thanks @​antoniovj1
  • remove return tags in TemplateAnnotationToThisRenderRector as no longer needed (#788)
  • return array if is array type in TemplateAnnotationToThisRenderRector (#786)
  • Bump to php-parser 5.5.0 and update fixture attributes usage (#784)
